item.title: See my review of that skip
item.content:encoded: Hijacking one skip's thread to discuss another in-depth would be rude and unfair of me…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-1048 seemed cute and friendly, too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mmmmm, hugs…why would the Foundation want to keep civiliians off such a harmless, comforting site? (The hugging entity can easily be explained away as augmented reality and haptic sensors on thesite servers.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very well done. And I pity poor Nahla as well

item.title: Re: I didn't think much at first.
item.content:encoded:
I just saw your comment today.

Oh my, thank you for making it one of your faves!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! Glad you liked how minimalistic it was.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this minimalist story telling technique. Very cool, I love articles that play with the form. +1

item.title: I didn't think much at first.
item.content:encoded:
I hit random SCP as I normally do, and thought this looked kinda short and i'd be on to the next in no time.

Gotta be honest, this is one of my favorite SCPs I've read. Even though it's not extremely detailed like others, it packs an emotional punch I didn't expect. At first I didn't understand but then I started looking closer and I almost cried.

I'm so glad I came across this SCP. +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *hands over tissues*

item.title: Re: Thanks, I hate it
item.content:encoded: Glad you liked it. I forgot where I saw/read this but when suicide comes into the talk I always remember this line "It won't take away the pain, you're only giving it to someone else". Hang in there peeps.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm bawling

+1

item.title: Thanks, I hate it
item.content:encoded:
You made me cry you son of a bitch, the moral part of me hates this but I still have to +1 it because it did it's job ;_;

I've seen people be down, want to end it for themselves, and I've personally dealt with similar stuff, though not super bad. This just… hurts…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Glad you find it so.

Thanks for the +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
God, that log of hugs is so heart-wrenching.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

I'm afraid you're too late though :(

Thanks for the upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want to hug anthony miles :(
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Are you perhaps a North Texas local missing a friend who moved to Maine?

Unfortunately, I'm not. I've pmed you the rest of my reply.

Thanks for checking out my SCP though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for your upvote ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey OP? Are you perhaps a North Texas local missing a friend who moved to Maine? Because if not, the fact that you're using my brother's name as your main hug-recipient is a hell of a coincidence. Feel free to DM me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: hoh, not the ending i expected but the one i upvoted for

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like I’m reading a joke with no punchline.

Huh. Since its posting until now, you're the only person so far that seems to think of this. Maybe perhaps another read may make it clearer?

It just seems somewhat purposeless if that make sense.

It honestly doesn't make sense to me. It's not even supposed to be somewhat slightly funny in the first place. Furthermore, I think its depth as it is is already sufficient, enough that it is not telling you exactly what is what but enough for the reader to figure it out.

But I feel like it has potential so no vote for now

Glad you think it has potential. Thanks for checking it out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I’m not sure how I feel about this one. I feel like I’m reading a joke with no punchline. It just seems somewhat purposeless if that make sense. I think it needs some sort of depth in some way.

But I feel like it has potential so no vote for now

item.title: Re: Not suited for Dummies
item.content:encoded:
OPEN [SPOILER]
CLOSE

When I said "footnotes", I was referring to the Updates Logs at the bottom of the the page.

I see. You could just call them as "Addendum" or "Updates" since "footnotes" means a very specific thing here.

I'm also a bit confused as to what exactly it means by "failed" under the delivery section,

"It" failed to deliver the hug?

as well as why Anthony Miles is considered so important to the Skip.

Just to be clear, the skip loves everyone, except for those trolls obviously.
If you look at the table again, you'll notice that Anthony Miles is the most frequent requestor. This, along with the types of hugs he requested and his comments were supposed to imply the condition he is currently in. The skip suposedly "liked" giving these hugs and surely it has noticed Anthony Miles and also possibly the condition he is in, hence why it repeatedly tried giving those hugs he previously requested.

And why exactly has it been unable to "meet" demands since the Foundation attempted to "kill" the SCP, when the Foundation eventually restored the website?

The Foundation did not attempt to kill the SCP. They just made the website inaccesible to the civilians. As to why its hugs "Failed" afterward, this is another implication to the current condition Anthonyy Miles is in. The "Out of Range" is a really big clue to what eventually happened to Anthony.

I'm not going to directly explain the meaning of those "Updates" so that it would not conflict with other people's personal explanation for it. We know that the skip will enter a refractory period after delivering a hug. This period previously lasted for 24 to 145 hours but in the "Updates" it says that this period is getting shorter and shorter until it is eventually reduced to less than 24 hours. This change occurred right after that "thing" with Anthony Miles.

Now why do some things enter a refractory period or a cool down period after doing something?

Let's say the skip is truly a benevolent being that really liked giving those much-desired hugs. How do you think will a being of this nature respond to what happened to Anthony Miles? Surely the skip would not want something like that to happen again, would it?

item.title: Re: Not suited for Dummies
item.content:encoded:
Alright, that was bad wording.
When I said "footnotes", I was referring to the Updates Logs at the bottom of the the page.
I'm also a bit confused as to what exactly it means by "failed" under the delivery section, as well as why Anthony Miles is considered so important to the Skip. And why exactly has it been unable to "meet" demands since the Foundation attempted to "kill" the SCP, when the Foundation eventually restored the website?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I added "i1l4y3" at the end of the domain name. Maybe "ily" is better? I don't know. Anyway, thank you for checking this out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Am I behind the times, or does this need something after the domain name? Do sites not need .com (or .whatever because I know there's a bunch of options) any more to function? Anyways either the internet has changed in a way I haven't noticed yet, or you need a *.something* whether you think that's an org, a com, a net, or even a fun international code.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad you liked it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll gladly take your +1. Thanks for reading it!

item.title: Re: Not suited for Dummies
item.content:encoded:
What do you mean by footnotes? Aren't footnotes this1What specific parts do you need explaining?

Footnotes
1. ? This a what a footnote is, right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is such a great short SCP that hits me right in the feels, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You wrote a SCP about a vague, blurry creature which emerges from computer screens and gives people hugs, and managed to make me feel very, very sad by the end.

Please, take my +1

item.title: Not suited for Dummies
item.content:encoded: Can someone explain this a bit to me? I understand that it's a "being" that comes from computers to give people hugs based on the type of hug requested, and that it cannot be apprehended, but everything else is confusing to me. What do the logs have to do with the footnotes? Am I missing something here?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh thank you for those kind words!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This kind of balance is hard to achieve; equal parts unsettling, heartwarming, and upsetting. You achieve this without having to employ typical empathetic hooks like children, animals, themes of abuse, etc. You just tell the story in a clever way. Well done. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you and thanks for reading it!

item.title: Re: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded:
Glad you liked it!

Continue
Close

The image caption is a little long, as it makes the text slightly too small for me to read easily. Maybe cut down on some of it?

Done.

Technically, web crawler is two words, however I think I have seen it written as one word in a few SCPs. Also, as far as I know, the web crawler itself wouldn't be capable of preventing access to the website. That would be a job for a human or another automated system.

Hmmm, since it seems that at the moment it's only been you that pointed that out, I should just keep the web crawler stuff. If I do need to change it, will "Foundation 'HORUS' automated system is to…" suffice?

This is one of the more unique ways of naming the action of applying amnestics. I'm familiar with amnesticised, amnesticized and amnestized, however I may have just missed other SCPs that use this spelling. I do prefer those other ones though.

I have changed it.

This sentence runs on a little too much, and uses 'and' three times. Maybe consider separating it into two sentences?

I actually think this can just be completely cut. In the table it's obvious what default means, so this sentence doesn't really add anything.

I'm not entirely sure that the "" around some words are needed. I'm less sure about some of the words, but I don't think they're needed around requestor.

The commas after specifically and rabbit are not needed.

Done.

Does this mean requests made in this time fail, or does it just not take them? If the former, nothing needs changing. If the latter, you need to add something about how some requests fail.

It's the former.

Should be SCP-4686-1

Should be a comma after failed. Also, either replace the second use of 'the entity' with 'it', or replace the first use of it with 'SCP-4686-1'

You can probably remove the 'within an unknown area around itself' clause. Also, maybe change the final section a little, because at the moment it seems like it corrupts all footage on the device, not just footage of itself. Whether this was your intention or not, I much prefer it just affecting pictures and videos of itself.

Done.

This should probably be 'Failed'. If this was a conscious choice to use denied over failed for this request, I'm not a fan. It seems to be an attempt to add humour to an entirely non-humorous article.

It wasn't an attempt in humor. I was trying to show that the entity takes its "job" "seriously".

I prefer something like "The Foundation's latest attempt at shutting down SCP-4686. The website remained accessible but SCP-4686-1 was not able to manifest."

Again, I think I'd namedrop SCP-4686 in this.

Replace this with either 'reduced by half', or 'reduced to half of its original length'.

Thinner probably doesn't need the quotation marks. Maybe replace it with slimmer? That's probably not necessary though.

Done.

Thank you for this! I can finally smoothen some of those rough edges.
Thank you for your critique and, again, glad you liked it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This kind of anomaly is very reminiscent to that of www.floatationdevice in a good way. It captures that sense of weirdness without having to rely on other factors like horror or humor to stand out. Good work.

item.title: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded:
Alright, let's do this:

+ Line by Line
– hide block

The image caption is a little long, as it makes the text slightly too small for me to read easily. Maybe cut down on some of it?

Foundation webcrawlers are to monitor SCP-4686 and prevent civilian access to the website.

Technically, web crawler is two words, however I think I have seen it written as one word in a few SCPs. Also, as far as I know, the web crawler itself wouldn't be capable of preventing access to the website. That would be a job for a human or another automated system.

Civilians who have accessed SCP-4686 are to be amnesticed.

This is one of the more unique ways of naming the action of applying amnestics. I'm familiar with amnesticised, amnesticized and amnestized, however I may have just missed other SCPs that use this spelling. I do prefer those other ones though.

has three fields

I think you should specify that these are "input fields".

When a person (the "requestor") fills the first two fields accordingly and clicks the “Request Hug” button, an entity referred to as SCP-4686-1 will emerge and grow from the monitor of the "requestor’s" device and proceed to embrace said "requestor".

This sentence runs on a little too much, and uses 'and' three times. Maybe consider separating it into two sentences?

If left blank it will automatically be filled with “DEFAULT”

I actually think this can just be completely cut. In the table it's obvious what default means, so this sentence doesn't really add anything.

I'm not entirely sure that the "" around some words are needed. I'm less sure about some of the words, but I don't think they're needed around requestor.

closely resembling that of a rabbit or a dog, specifically, an Angora rabbit, or a Samoyed dog.

The commas after specifically and rabbit are not needed.

The website will then enter a refractory period lasting from 24 to 145 hours where it will be unavailable to take in requests.

Does this mean requests made in this time fail, or does it just not take them? If the former, nothing needs changing. If the latter, you need to add something about how some requests fail.

SCP-4686 will wave one of its limbs

Should be SCP-4686-1

Any attempt to apprehend the entity has failed as the entity is capable of becoming incorporeal.

Should be a comma after failed. Also, either replace the second use of 'the entity' with 'it', or replace the first use of it with 'SCP-4686-1'

The entity also affects any recording devices pointing at itself, within an unknown area around itself, distorting or corrupting any data within these devices.

You can probably remove the 'within an unknown area around itself' clause. Also, maybe change the final section a little, because at the moment it seems like it corrupts all footage on the device, not just footage of itself. Whether this was your intention or not, I much prefer it just affecting pictures and videos of itself.

Denied

This should probably be 'Failed'. If this was a conscious choice to use denied over failed for this request, I'm not a fan. It seems to be an attempt to add humour to an entirely non-humorous article.

Foundation latest attempt at shutting down the website. Website remained accessible but SCP-4686-1 was not able to manifest.

I prefer something like "The Foundation's latest attempt at shutting down SCP-4686. The website remained accessible but SCP-4686-1 was not able to manifest."

Website returned to its normal operations.

Again, I think I'd namedrop SCP-4686 in this.

SCP-4686’s refractory period has been observed to be reduced in half.

Replace this with either 'reduced by half', or 'reduced to half of its original length'.

has started to appear “thinner” than usual

Thinner probably doesn't need the quotation marks. Maybe replace it with slimmer? That's probably not necessary though.

– hide block

Overall, I really liked it. Not going to give any spoilers out, but when I realised what had happened it really packed an emotional punch. The table is short enough to be easily readable, whilst not being too short to convey the narrative as well as it does. Despite the slight roughness around the edges of this SCP, I was very happy to give it a +1.

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to gee0765 or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really sorry about those rough edges. Glad it wasn't bad enough for you not to like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is undeniably rough around the edges and can def use some tweaking, but jesus christ that log got a very visceral reaction out of me.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Glad you enjoyed it!

Edit: Like what I said to NatVoltaic, sorry about those rough edges. Glad you liked the logs. Thank you.

item.title: Re: Pleasantly surprised.
item.content:encoded:
Yey! Glad you liked it.

About your Layout Nitpick
Close

I've swapped "acquired" and "removed". About the amnesticization, I think I'm keeping it that way because I was thinking it was just some sort of extra precaution because even if SCP-XXXX-1 did not manifest, the person still knows the website and he/she might share it with their friends like "Hey, there was this weird website…" and their friends might get interested and then they may visit the website and SCP-XXXX-1 may manifest on their turn.

Anyway, thanks for reading it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed that far more than I expected myself to.

To expand: it's a tad rough around the edges but I love the conceit of the Foundation ruining this thing, and the use of the logs as a tool for pathos is really tight. Really well done.

item.title: Pleasantly surprised.
item.content:encoded:
+ My take on this
- Sadder than I thought

At first I thought this was a silly concept, since my mind went straight to SCP-\̅\̅\̅\̅-J and the "This is strictly limited to researchers who wish to be held" footnote. I was going to give it a no-vote but I wanted to go back and re-read it first.

I got to the last block of entries again and this time I noticed what was going on, with the site itself being the requester and the name of the intended recipient. Then the footnotes…not going to lie, for how simple and straightforward this seems it still hit me on an emotional level I wasn't expecting.

So, have a +1, I need to go hug my dog.

+ Layout nitpick
- Close

I feel like in the second sentence of the containment procedures 'removed' and 'acquired' should be swapped, so the information is copied before removal. Also, amnesticizing everyone who accessed the site seems excessive, maybe limit it to the users who actually encountered a manifestation of SCP-4686-1?

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Thank you to the following people for giving their critique on this SCP:
(In alphabetical order)
Ayers (the "User Response" was his idea)
Crashington
gee0765
Laneous
N_Aepic_Fael
RadioactiveRADS
RockTeethMothEyes
TheMightyMcB

Also, thank you to these following people:
AbsentmindedNihilist and Djoric or was it [[Anthenodora]], for telling me that that "thing" was called an "input field". [[riyangendut]] and [[NamelessMediocre]] for checking out the image. DrAkimoto, Anticlea Langstrom, and silentsquirrel for their additional critique.

The image is an edited/modified combination of these three images:

1. "Bear hug"
Link: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bear_hug_(6360447621).jpg

2. "Child hugging tree at Peravoor"
Link: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Child_hugging_tree_at_Peravoor_(6).jpg

3. "Deer at Night"
Link: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Deer_at_Night_(11425649114).jpg

Edit: I've only discovered today that this SCP was actually translated into Japanese by C-Dives and Chinese by Eurya. Thank you both for translating it!
